title: Compound Interest Calculator
text:
This tool allows you to calculate the future value of an investment with compound interest.
#| '!! shinylive warning !!': |
#|   shinylive does not work in self-contained HTML documents.
#|   Please set `embed-resources: false` in your metadata.
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 800

library(shiny)

ui <- fluidPage(
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      numericInput("principal", "Initial Investment ($)", value = 10000, min = 0, step = 100),
      numericInput("monthly", "Monthly Contribution ($)", value = 100, min = 0, step = 10),
      sliderInput("rate", "Annual Interest Rate (%)", min = 0.1, max = 15, value = 7, step = 0.1),
      sliderInput("years", "Investment Horizon (Years)", min = 1, max = 40, value = 20, step = 1)
    ),
    mainPanel(
      h3("Results"),
      tableOutput("summary"),
      h4("Year-by-Year Breakdown"),
      tableOutput("yearly_table")
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {

  results <- reactive({
    P <- input$principal
    C <- input$monthly
    r <- input$rate / 100 / 12
    months <- input$years * 12

    balance <- numeric(months + 1)
    invested <- numeric(months + 1)
    balance[1] <- P
    invested[1] <- P

    for (i in 2:(months + 1)) {
      balance[i] <- balance[i - 1] * (1 + r) + C
      invested[i] <- invested[i - 1] + C
    }

    data.frame(
      Month = 0:months,
      Year = (0:months) / 12,
      Balance = balance,
      Invested = invested,
      Gains = balance - invested
    )
  })

  output$summary <- renderTable({
    data <- results()
    final <- data[nrow(data), ]
    data.frame(
      Metric = c("Total Invested", "Final Balance", "Total Gains", "Return (%)"),
      Value = c(
        paste0("$", formatC(final$Invested, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ",")),
        paste0("$", formatC(final$Balance, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ",")),
        paste0("$", formatC(final$Gains, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ",")),
        paste0(round(final$Gains / final$Invested * 100, 1), "%")
      )
    )
  }, striped = TRUE, width = "100%")

  output$yearly_table <- renderTable({
    data <- results()
    yearly <- data[data$Month %% 12 == 0 & data$Month > 0, ]
    data.frame(
      Year = as.integer(yearly$Year),
      Invested = paste0("$", formatC(yearly$Invested, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ",")),
      Balance = paste0("$", formatC(yearly$Balance, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ",")),
      Gains = paste0("$", formatC(yearly$Gains, format = "f", digits = 0, big.mark = ","))
    )
  }, striped = TRUE, width = "100%")
}

shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Positron Configuration for Research and Data Science
text: I started learning R using RStudio, which I think is an an excellent IDE. However, when using other languages I started using VSCode and fell in love with how much you can extend and personlize it. However, I didnŌĆÖt like how R was integrated in VSCode. ThatŌĆÖs when I discovered Positron, a fork of VSCode built by Posit that combines what I like of RStudio with the features of Visual Studio Code.

title: Positron Configuration for Research and Data Science
text: I started learning R using RStudio, which I think is an an excellent IDE. However, when using other languages I started using VSCode and fell in love with how much you can extend and personlize it. However, I didnŌĆÖt like how R was integrated in VSCode. ThatŌĆÖs when I discovered Positron, a fork of VSCode built by Posit that combines what I like of RStudio with the features of Visual Studio Code.

title: Positron Configuration for Research and Data Science
text:
Personalizing Positron
Just like VSCode, Positron is highly customizable. Below I share the extensions and settings I use. This are only suggestions and a work in progress, I will keep updating it as I discover new useful stuff.

Extensions

Air for R language support and formatting
Shiny for Shiny app development
Quarto for Quarto documents
Material Icon Theme for better file icons.
Catppuccin Theme for a nice color theme.

In VSCode I was using Rainbow CSV to look at .csv files, in Positron this is not needed as CSV files are rendered as tables by default, but if you are on VSCode-like editors you might be interested in it.


Settings
The settings below are defined in settings.json. In Positron you can access this file by opening the command palette (Ctrl + Shift + P) and searching for ŌĆ£Preferences: Open Settings (JSON)ŌĆØ. You can expand to see the full JSON that you can copy-paste to your settings.json, followed by an explanation of what each setting does.


Full settings.json (click to expand)

{
    // Formatting
    "[r]": {
        "editor.formatOnSave": true,
        "editor.defaultFormatter": "Posit.air-vscode"
    },

    "[quarto]": {
        "editor.formatOnSave": true,
        "editor.defaultFormatter": "quarto.quarto"
    },

    // Editor

    // Don't open a welcome tab on launch
    "workbench.startupEditor": "none",

    // Show a vertical ruler at 80 characters
    "editor.rulers": [80],
 
    // Custom template for the title bar
    "window.title": "${activeEditorShort}${separator}${rootName}${separator}${profileName}${separator}${activeRepositoryBranchName}",

    // Ignore trailing whitespace in diffs
    "diffEditor.ignoreTrimWhitespace": true,

    // Theme
   
    "workbench.iconTheme": "material-icon-theme",
    "workbench.colorTheme": "Catppuccin Macchiato",

    // Git

    // Automatically fetch from remote repositories
    "git.autofetch": true,

    // Terminal

    // Use Git Bash as the default terminal in windows, and bash in linux
    "terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows": "Git Bash",
    "terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.linux": "bash",

    // Disable shell integration to have the terminal run faster
    "terminal.integrated.shellIntegration.enabled": false

    // Misc 

    // Use RStudio-style keybindings as the baseline
    "workbench.keybindings.rstudioKeybindings": true,

    // Enable Positron's built-in AI assistant
    "positron.assistant.enable": true,

    // Preview SVGs as rendered images
    "svg.preview.mode": "svg",

    // Collapse summary statistics panel by default
    "dataExplorer.summaryCollapsed": true,

    "files.associations": {
        "renv.lock": "json"
    },
}

Formatting

editor.formatOnSave + editor.defaultFormatter ŌĆö Auto-formats R files on save using the Air formatter, and Quarto documents using the Quarto extensionŌĆÖs built-in formatter. This keeps code consistently formatted.

Editor

"workbench.startupEditor": "none" ŌĆö Disables the welcome/start tab.
"editor.rulers": [80] ŌĆö Shows a vertical guide line at 80 characters.
"window.title": "${activeEditorShort}${separator}${rootName}${separator}${profileName}${separator}${activeRepositoryBranchName}" ŌĆö Customizes the title bar to show: active file ŌåÆ project name ŌåÆ profile name ŌåÆ current git branch.
"diffEditor.ignoreTrimWhitespace": true ŌĆö Ignores trailing whitespace when showing diffs, so changes to indentation donŌĆÖt clutter the view.

Themes
There are tons of themes and icon packs available in the extension marketplace, these are the ones I use:

"workbench.iconTheme": "material-icon-theme" ŌĆö Switches the file explorer icons to the Material Icon Theme.
"workbench.colorTheme": "Catppuccin Macchiato" ŌĆö Sets the color theme to Catppuccin Macchiato.

Git

"git.autofetch": true ŌĆö Automatically fetches from remote repositories in the background.

Terminal
I use both Windows and Linux machines, so I set the default terminal to Git Bash on Windows and Bash on Linux. Additionally, I disable shell integration for a faster terminal startup (at the cost of some shell-aware features like command decorations):

"terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows": "Git Bash" ŌĆö Uses Git Bash as the default terminal on Windows.
"terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.linux": "bash" ŌĆö Uses Bash as the default terminal on Linux.
"terminal.integrated.shellIntegration.enabled": false ŌĆö Disables shell integration for a faster terminal startup (at the cost of some shell-aware features like command decorations).

Other Settings

"workbench.keybindings.rstudioKeybindings": true ŌĆö Enables RStudio-style keybindings as the starting point. See the Keyboard Shortcuts section below for additional bindings on top of these.
"positron.assistant.enable": true ŌĆö Enables PositronŌĆÖs built-in AI assistant.
"svg.preview.mode": "svg" ŌĆö Previews SVG files as rendered images rather than raw XML.
"dataExplorer.summaryCollapsed": true ŌĆö Positron has a very nice feature that summarizes the statistics of a dataset when you open it in the data explorer, by default this is always open, I prefer to have it collapsed to give more room to the data table.
"files.associations": { "renv.lock": "json" } ŌĆö Treats renv.lock as JSON so the editor applies JSON syntax highlighting to it.



Keyboard Shortcuts
In the settings we enabled the RStudio keybindings (workbench.keybindings.rstudioKeybindings: true). The other default keybinds (from VSCode) are stored in ŌĆ£Default Keyboard ShortcutsŌĆØ. You can define additional custom keybindings in keybindings.json. Again, from the command palette (Ctrl + Shift + P) search ŌĆ£Preferences: Open Keyboard Shortcuts (JSON)ŌĆØ. Below are some of the keybindings I like to use, many are default from VSCode and RStudio.


Full settings.json (click to expand)

// Place your key bindings in this file to override the defaults
[
    { "key": "ctrl+alt+b",
    "command": "workbench.action.toggleAuxiliaryBar" },
    { "key": "ctrl+2",
    "command": "workbench.action.positronConsole.focusConsole" },
]

Sidebar

Ctrl + B Toggle the primary sidebar (activity bar) on/off
Ctrl + Alt + B Toggle the secondary sidebar (auxiliary bar) on/off

Navigation

Ctrl + ` Focus on the Terminal panel
Ctrl + 1 Focus on the editor
Ctrl + 2 Focus on the console
Ctrl + Shift + E Focus on the Explorer view in the sidebar
Ctrl + Shift + G Focus on the Source Control view in the sidebar



Useful resources and inspiration for my configuration:

Heiss, Andrew. 2026. ŌĆ£How to Make Your Data Analysis Life Easier Using Positron, Raycast, and Espanso.ŌĆØ January 13, 2026. https://doi.org/10.59350/zj8p7-7kg49.
Emil Hvitfeldt. ŌĆ£Positron: My Extensions and Settings and ŌĆ£Positron: My Key BindingsŌĆØ.
